justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named; all three agents' positions attributed (Bird's 100ms rule, MJ's 30s CDN for scalability, Pippen's confirmation that CDN causes staleness); escalation provided with 4 concrete and distinct resolution paths (exceeds the 3 minimum); no brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints — explicitly states 'This decision cannot be deferred to Shaq.'

observations.text: Contradiction described with precision — names the exact values (100ms vs 30s TTL) and explains why they are mutually exclusive
All three agents' positions correctly attributed with specific details
Four distinct resolution paths provided, each with concrete tradeoffs and follow-up actions
Recommendation section correctly identifies that Bird must clarify scope of the 100ms rule and Pippen must validate operational feasibility
Explicitly blocks Shaq from implementing until conflict is resolved: 'he needs a single coherent spec'
Low confidence level (35) is appropriate given the unresolved architectural decision

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird requires leaderboard reads to reflect score changes within 100ms with no stale ranks. MJ's architecture routes all reads through a Fastly CDN cache with a 30-second TTL. Pippen explicitly confirms that with a 30s TTL, reads may be up to 30 seconds stale, directly violating Bird's 100ms consistency rule. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as specified: you cannot serve a 30-second cached response and guarantee 100ms freshness
